subject: Cloud Computing
title: File Storage in the Cloud
description: A fast breakdown of File Storage in the Cloud for builders.

hook.text: Your team needs a shared drive that multiple servers can access simultaneously. File storage does that.

buildup.text: Cloud file storage — AWS EFS, Azure Files — provides shared directories accessible from many machines at once.

discovery.text: It uses familiar protocols like NFS and SMB. Applications see it as a normal network drive.

twist.text: File storage costs more per gigabyte than object storage. Companies that use it for archival waste money.

climax.text: Use file storage when multiple servers must share files. Use object storage for everything else.

punchline.text: Shared files across servers. That's file storage's job.

quiz.question: When should you use file storage over object storage?
options: When multiple servers need to access shared files simultaneously
When storing large video files for archival purposes
When you need the cheapest possible storage option
